text: delegate codex lanes failed twice mid-build (work-account quota, then an expired restored token) but both surfaced as generic harness_error; a quota/auth-specific failure reason would have saved ~30 minutes of diagnosis
gh repo fork --clone treated an explicit destination path as a repository and failed after creating the remote fork; use fork without clone followed by an explicit git clone
crates.io API returned HTTP 503 while checking whether the papercuts package namespace is owned; retry with an identified API client and treat registry ownership as unverified until a successful read
the expected target/release/papercuts binary was absent during a read-only schema probe; use cargo run or build the release binary before invoking that path

the destructive-command guard treated an angle-bracket path placeholder inside a Beads note as shell redirection and blocked the entire safe metadata update; avoid angle-bracket placeholders in shell arguments or submit metadata through a non-shell API
a disposable Git probe combined an expected failing rev-parse with set -e inside command substitution and produced empty exit-status evidence; capture expected failures with an explicit if branch
zsh reserves the variable name status as read-only, so an expected-failure probe aborted when assigning status=0; use rc or exit_code for portable shell snippets

apply_patch rejected a safe documentation refinement because the expected context split a wrapped paragraph differently from the file; inspect the exact numbered lines before retrying a narrower patch
a broad angle-bracket placeholder check matched the legitimate Rust type Option<String> in an ADR and stopped validation early; scope placeholder lint to shell code blocks or known redirect patterns
Source inspection assumed src/model.rs, but this repository keeps record types elsewhere; a short source layout note or module map would prevent the failed read.
A direct jq filter assumed br list --json returned a top-level issue array, but this br version wraps or reshapes the result; document the JSON envelope or provide a stable robot query example.
Overlapping sed ranges made ADR headings appear duplicated in review output, leading to a failed cleanup patch; use non-overlapping ranges or numbered context for long-document review.
